text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Most program managers and health care workers perceived information from WHO, NCDC, and NPHCDA as reliable because it is evidence‑based","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Most program managers and HCW perceived information from WHO, Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC), and National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA) as reliable because it is evidence-based, and these agencies were responsible for handling the crisis.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that program managers and health care workers view information from those agencies as reliable due to its evidence‑based nature.","population":"program managers and health care workers","intervention":"information from WHO, NCDC, and NPHCDA","comparator":"","outcome":"perceived as reliable","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Most community members regarded information received from radio and television as trustworthy","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Most community members regarded information received from the radio and television as trustworthy, while a few thought the same about information from the WHO and NCDC.","entailment":"The quote explicitly says that the majority of community members consider radio and television information trustworthy.","population":"community members","intervention":"information from radio and television","comparator":"","outcome":"regarded as trustworthy","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Most participants believed social media information is unreliable because it is subjective and open for anybody to publish","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Most participants believed social media information is unreliable because it is subjective and open for anybody to publish.","entailment":"The sentence directly reports participants' belief that social media information lacks reliability due to its subjectivity and open nature.","population":"participants","intervention":"social media information","comparator":"","outcome":"believed to be unreliable","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Some people do not believe COVID‑19 exists because they have not personally experienced a case or death","claimType":"empirical","quote":"participants reported that some people did not believe in the existence of COVID-19 because they had not had a personal experience with a COVID-19 case or death.","entailment":"The quoted passage links lack of personal experience with the belief that COVID‑19 does not exist.","population":"people who do not believe COVID‑19 exists","intervention":"lack of personal experience with a COVID‑19 case or death","comparator":"","outcome":"do not believe COVID‑19 exists","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Belief that the pandemic response is a fraud is rooted in denial of the virus's existence and distrust of government integrity","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The belief that the pandemic response was a fraud, and a means for official graft, was rooted in the concurrent denial of the existence of the virus and distrust of the government’s integrity and intentions.","entailment":"The sentence states that the fraud belief stems from both denial of the virus and distrust of the government.","population":"","intervention":"belief that pandemic response is a fraud","comparator":"","outcome":"rooted in denial of virus existence and distrust of government integrity","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"}]}
